# Clergy-Witness Procedural Recital in Sealed Cannibal-Case Briefing

## What the Artifact Implies

The closed-room briefing passage in the artifact is unusual in its explicit register-naming: *"The senior clergy-witness spoke then and said the words I expected him to say, which were that the magistrate-docket classification of the interior find would be entered as grave-violation-of-person, that the access would be sealed to magistrate-warrant only, that the burial of the victims would be held under civic-oath with family rites permitted privately and public ceremony absent, and that the band's remaining sign would be burned at the ruin before the thaw-moon closed. He did not say this in the register of apology. He said it in the register of procedure. I took it in the same register."* Melissa surfaces two institutional facts simultaneously: first, that the senior clergy-witness performs a structured RECITAL naming classification + access-level + burial convention + cleanup-timing in a specific sequence; second, that the recital operates in a "register of procedure" that is categorically distinct from a "register of apology" and is recognized by experienced contractor-witnesses. The register-naming IS the convention the artifact proposes — "the words I expected him to say" indicates that Melissa, rotating across chartered polities, has encountered this form before.

## Why It Fits This World

Animalia's Charter-Era civic framework rests on multispecies civic-registry infrastructure where common-pantheon clergy aspectualize progenitor-cult positions (CF-0016) and function as legitimation partners to civic-charter authority. CF-0036 commits the silence-protocol, the sealed-docket procedure, and the civic-clerical witness role; CF-0033 (via CH-0010) explicitly reserves sealed-docket cannibal-case procedure to civic-magistrate and civic-watch, firewalling dragoon-chaplain involvement. What the existing canon does not commit is the performative register the clergy-witness uses when performing the silence-protocol-invocation rite. The artifact fills that gap with a register that is exactly right for Animalia's bureaucratic-drift civic-aesthetic: procedural, not apologetic; institutional-cadence, not moral-confrontation; sealing the case in the same register the magistrate's second's re-classification notice uses. The register discipline parallels CF-0033's civic-suppression-as-bureaucratic-drift commitment and CF-0036's register-of-procedure load-bearing stabilizer. It fits the world's aesthetic because Animalia's civic-clerical authority is CHARTERED CIVIC-PARTNER, not pastoral-intercessor — the procedural-register recital is the exact institutional shape that commitment produces.

## Narrator Reliability Assessment

Melissa "Threadscar" (CHAR-0002) is firsthand and central; she was present in the closed-room briefing as contractor of record. Her AES-1 scarred-veteran cadence (per CHAR-0002 Voice and Perception) gives her the register-discrimination competence to name the distinction between procedure and apology, and the phrase "the words I expected him to say" indicates she has recognized the recital-form across rotations. Evidence-breadth search confirms CF-0016 / CF-0024 / CF-0036 together establish the institutional framework within which a senior clergy-witness would speak, but none commits the specific recital-form or the register distinction. Phase 6d.1 therefore requires soft_canon. Scope is regional (adopting polities per CF-0036 silence-protocol availability) and restricted-group (closed-room briefing participants only — contractor-witnesses, magistrate's second, posting-clerk's senior), because the recital is not public-register practice; it is the sealed-docket moment's civic-clerical interface.

## Immediate Consequences

CF-0036 silence-protocol application gains a concrete ritual form at the chartered-body level. Senior clergy-witnesses in adopting polities have a named civic-procedural role alongside hearth-prayer and legitimation functions — a third clergy role. The register-of-procedure vs register-of-apology distinction becomes legible to contractor-witnesses and civic officials as the institutional cue that the case is in-protocol; a clergy-witness who drifts into apologetic register is signaling that the case has NOT cleared the civic classification process. Contractor-witnesses learn to take the recital same-register, permitting the civic interface to close without interpretive strain and without risking the contractor being read as public-register dissident. Aspectualizing common-pantheon clergy (CF-0016 mainstream) gain a concrete procedural competency distinct from pastoral ministry; reform-current clergy (CF-0026 CH-0010 bifurcation) gain a new debate surface.

## Longer-Term Consequences

Civic-clerical training in adopting polities develops a recital-register pedagogy: new senior clergy-witnesses learn bureaucratic-drift cadence as a distinct competency alongside hearth-prayer and funerary rites. This parallels CF-0033's civic-watch register-discipline and extends it from civic-watch into clergy territory, producing a generational shift in how common-pantheon aspectualizing clergy interface with civic-suppression procedure. Bardic-circuit contracted-bard narrative-custody (CF-0017 extension per CF-0024) develops a recital-cadence subgenre within the silenced-contractor ballad register — the itinerant bard cites the register distinction as cadence marker; the breach-register sectarian-commissioned bard inflates the recital INTO pastoral or prophetic register to recruit for supremacist doctrine, and listeners mark the breach per CF-0017 CH-0010. CF-0026 principled-refuser clergy bifurcation gains a concrete debate surface: charter-defense-anti-supremacism wing objects that procedure-register recital cooperates in civic-suppression without adequate moral witness; civic-restraint-pragmatic wing defends the register as civic-charter duty. Magistrate-docket LB-6 (clergy bifurcation case law per CF-0036 notes) accumulates procedural-recital disputes as a named sub-category. Civic-registry archival practice catalogs recital dates and attending officiants, producing per-polity civic-clerical activity trails that reform-current clergy can cite in LB-1 unsealing-petition campaigns.

## Risks

Primary Rule-3 risk: the procedural recital must NOT be inflated into mystical, prophetic, revelatory, or pastoral-intercession form. The register-of-procedure stabilizer IS the firewall — canon-addition adjudication must preserve bureaucratic-drift cadence explicitly and forbid drift toward cosmic-register or oracle-clergy inflation (M-7 holding clause extension risk). Secondary Rule-7 risk: the ritual form's antiquity is M-18's bounded unknown (Charter-Era founding-compromise vs Incident Wave response vs Layer-4 consolidation all remain contested); canon-addition must commit CURRENT practice only, no antiquity claim. Tertiary M-5 firewall: the clergy-witness role in sealed cannibal-case must not drift toward sentience-boundary adjudication; the recital's naming of grave-violation-of-person classification preserves SOC-1 by refusing the sectarian "mere beasts" reading (CF-0036 FIFTH M-5 cross-application), and the procedural register must continue to honor that firewall. Integration burden is moderate because the card touches RELIGION, INSTITUTIONS, and LAW — canon-addition should verify cross-domain coherence.

## Likely Burden If Accepted

Moderate. `INSTITUTIONS.md` §Religion gains the civic-procedural-witness role as a third named common-pantheon aspectualizing clergy competency, with bureaucratic-drift cadence training noted as a distinct pedagogical discipline. §Law/Custom/Judgment gains the procedural-recital moment as a formal step in CF-0036 sealed-docket application. `CANON_LEDGER.md` receives the new CF with cross-reference annotations to CF-0016, CF-0024, CF-0026, CF-0033, CF-0036.

## Likely Story Yield

Strong. Story engines activated: a senior clergy-witness who breaks into apologetic register at a recital, signaling moral dissent within the institutional frame; a contractor who hears the recital and takes the register wrong (slipping into pastoral reception), marking himself as first-rotation in the closed-room; a reform-current clergyman who files charter-court challenges against his own institution's procedural-register training after a traumatic recital; an itinerant bard's silenced-contractor ballad that cites the register distinction as refrain and draws sectarian attention; a civic-registry archivist who finds per-polity recital-frequency data and begins quietly compiling it for reform-current access.

## Would This Be Better As

`soft_canon` as proposed. Regional scope is intrinsic (CF-0036 silence-protocol is polity-asymmetrically adopted; CF-0016 civic-clerical form varies). Social scope is restricted-group (closed-room briefing only). Promotion to hard_canon would violate 6d.1 evidence-breadth. Demotion to contested_canon would misread bureaucratic-drift institutional practice as disputed.

## Canon Safety Check Trace

**Phase 6a (Invariants)**: All 16 invariants tested; zero violations. SOC-1 reinforced (grave-violation-of-person classification preserves sentient-victim status). AES-1 (bureaucratic-drift cadence) and AES-2 (ordinary-institutional register) respected. No engagement with ONT/CAU/DIS invariants.

**Phase 6b (Mystery Reserve)**: M-1 through M-18 checked. **M-18 OVERLAP NOTED** — the recital is PART of the CF-0036 silence-protocol procedure whose antiquity is M-18's bounded-unknown. PASS: the card commits CURRENT practice only; no antiquity claim (Charter-Era founding-compromise vs Incident Wave response vs Layer-4 consolidation all remain contested). The register-of-procedure distinction is current-application, not historical-origin. Other MR entries: no overlap; M-4/M-5/M-7/M-16 explicitly preserved (classification-via-SOC-1, no-oracle-inflation, no-doctrine-origin).

**Phase 6c (Distribution Discipline)**: CFs consulted: CF-0016 (Common Pantheon / Progenitor-Cult coexistence), CF-0024 (Charter Hall institutional infrastructure), CF-0026 (principled-refuser clergy bifurcation per CH-0010), CF-0033 (dragoon-scope firewall per CH-0010), CF-0036 (silence-protocol + register-discipline). `why_not_universal` cites polity-asymmetric silence-protocol adoption, regional CF-0016 variation, dragoon-polity CH-0010 firewall, and non-Charter-Hall intake-form absence.

**Phase 6d (Diegetic-to-World Laundering)**:
- **6d.1 Evidence-breadth**: artifact is sole source for the specific recital-form and register-distinction → soft_canon required → PASS.
- **6d.2 Epistemic-horizon**: Melissa's CF-0024 rotation experience and AES-1 register-discrimination competence per CHAR-0002 place the recital observation and register-naming within her plausible reach → PASS.
- **6d.3 MR Positional**: Melissa's position gives firsthand access to per-case recital practice; NOT to M-18 civic-institutional founding-documents, M-16 doctrine-antiquity, M-5 sentience-boundary cosmology, or M-7 clergy-reputation civic-archive. Her report is per-case observation in AES-1 register, not archival synthesis → PASS.

**Phase 6f Repairs Applied**: none fired.
